215918.explanation: It is a medieval measure, the proceeds of which go into general funds, raised solely from dog owners. Besides, we do not have a tax for horses or cats, which can also cause feces nuisance.

215920.explanation: The municipality should not interfere with this. It is up to the entrepreneur to decide whether or not to open the shop on Sundays.

215921.explanation: There is enough nature, so much that it can even be obstructive.

215922.explanation: WORTHLESS statement, because it is not correct. In Twenterand, NO PRIORITY is given to status holders. The figures and facts: in the past 5 years, on average 4-5% of social rental homes have gone to status holders, so 95-96% did not. So there is no question of 'continuing to give' priority. We do believe recognized refugees should have housing, but not with priority.

215923.explanation: Given the tight housing market, it is logical that companies that employ foreign workers for economic reasons also provide housing for these workers.

215924.explanation: Not all waste, but some are already free, such as GFT waste (vegetable, fruit, and garden waste), electronics, paint, spray cans.

215925.explanation: But then there must be enough staff, and there is not currently. The Social Workplace (Wsw) is coming to an end due to a decision taken in The Hague, which is one of the main causes.

215926.explanation: If the sound is too loud/high, which can cause hearing damage, the norm must be adjusted.

215927.explanation: If it concerns fallow land and within 1 or 2 hectares, it must be possible.

215928.explanation: This is already possible through a detour (regulation).

215929.explanation: That is a choice the school can make. They receive funding for physical education.

215930.explanation: It then concerns general musical education, such as recorder lessons and learning to read notes.

215932.explanation: It is mandatory, but you can question it.

215933.explanation: Depending on the environmental category and possible resale thereafter.

215934.explanation: This only applies to the 30 existing solitary recreational homes in our municipality.

215935.explanation: Raising the income threshold is a general measure. The important thing is to help those who need it. No child should grow up in poverty according to us. For that, we have a generous minimum policy with various schemes, more generous compared to many other municipalities in Overijssel. Every child must also be able to participate. That is why we want the Participation Scheme to return in a new form.

215937.explanation: Limits may be set. For example, remission of emptying the gray container with residual waste, maximum 6 times per year remission (the average per household). This is to prevent waste tourism.

215938.explanation: This also does not apply to people who work.

215939.explanation: But these charging points do not need to be the most prominent. Now they are located right near a store entrance, and we do not like this kind of 'priority'. What applies to one car ...

215940.explanation: You cannot state this in general terms. Access roads to areas are always 50 km/h.

215941.explanation: If a company has to apply for debt counseling, the company is effectively already bankrupt.

215942.explanation: Only if this fits within the existing applicable budgets.

215944.explanation: Already happens for girls/women with low income, among others through the Care Information Points in the 4 centers.

215945.explanation: We want to give village councils and business associations a stronger position.

215949.explanation: Provided it fits in the rural area, such as bed and breakfast, childcare, or farm shops.

215951.explanation: As long as it covers costs, it is not necessary. The system as it currently works is excellent. The municipality of Twenterand is the cheapest in Overijssel and surroundings.

215952.explanation: We are on schedule regarding housing permit holders and are doing extra for accommodating Ukrainians. Accommodation for asylum seekers is not an option, as not every asylum seeker is a refugee. Moreover, in the western part of the country, more than 100 municipalities do virtually nothing. It's their turn.

215953.explanation: We are on schedule regarding housing permit holders and are doing extra for accommodating Ukrainians. Accommodation for asylum seekers is not an option, as not every asylum seeker is a refugee. Moreover, in the western part of the country, more than 100 municipalities do virtually nothing. It's their turn.

215954.explanation: First a warning (yellow card), then at the second yellow card a red card, meaning a fine.

215955.explanation: Due to European regulations, a municipal ban is impossible.

215956.explanation: No tree planting just for planting’s sake. It must serve a purpose.

215957.explanation: It must comply with regulations. No exceptions.

215958.explanation: Depending on use, intensity, and safety.

215960.explanation: The current way is fine: occasional events are regularly subsidized.

215963.explanation: Banning everywhere goes too far. For example, at events, as long as it causes no nuisance. The General Local Regulation (APV) does allow area-specific bans aimed at preventing disturbance caused by public drunkenness.

215964.explanation: We have a housing shortage, so an instrument is needed to encourage property owners to rent out or sell their buildings. This can be done via a Vacancy Regulation.

215966.explanation: But the municipality is not a party to this.

215968.explanation: This is up to the province. They are the cause of the damage.

215971.explanation: Where it is possible, that is fine.

215973.explanation: Not the task of a youth worker. It is a specialty that belongs with, for example, Tactus.

215974.explanation: Only if a situation calls for it.

215976.explanation: The municipality has already expanded to 4 enforcement officers and that is enough.

215918.explanation: No additional burden for citizens. However, we do find it important that people take their own responsibility and clean up to prevent nuisance.

215920.explanation: A well-considered decision has been made to allow limited opening hours; everyone can make their own choice and assessment.

215921.explanation: We have sufficient nature areas with many special natural values.

215922.explanation: We are in favor of a good distribution of our homes and equal opportunities for everyone.

215923.explanation: We cannot place this responsibility solely on companies. We also have a responsibility. We must critically assess which companies we want to attract labor migrants for. National and European politics also have a large role here.

215924.explanation: Nothing is free. Waste processing costs money; we pay waste disposal charges for this. The CDA wants to keep these charges as low as possible.

215925.explanation: Neighborhoods and districts deserve a neat appearance. Leading by example encourages others to follow.

215926.explanation: The CDA wants to reduce regulatory pressure in society. The municipality makes a careful assessment when granting permits for festivals. Excessive noise nuisance must be prevented.

215927.explanation: Solar panels belong on roofs, and agricultural land is needed for food security.

215928.explanation: Every child should have the opportunity to play sports. In addition, it is positive for young people to belong somewhere.

215929.explanation: Swimming skills for our children are important. The CDA is open to initiatives for school swimming.

215930.explanation: The CDA wants sport and culture to be accessible to all children. We do this in cooperation with our associations.

215932.explanation: The local CDA wants to abide by the law; 30% is our minimum. We are in favor of a good mix of affordable owner-occupied and rental homes in different segments.

215934.explanation: This comes at the expense of recreation and tourism. We must commit to building more homes for our residents.

215935.explanation: People in poverty must be supported; the income limit does not need to be raised for this.

215936.explanation: If possible, that's good, but it does not work for everyone.

215937.explanation: The municipality must show its human side: waiving municipal taxes is one of the most effective measures municipalities can take to support people with low incomes.

215938.explanation: If these people cannot provide informal care, then expensive care must be used.

215939.explanation: The CDA finds it important that there are sufficient charging points for electric cars, which should be placed according to demand. Residents should be able to apply to the municipality. There must be sufficient parking for people who do not drive electric vehicles.

215940.explanation: Unless this is important for safety (e.g., school zones and shopping centers).

215942.explanation: The CDA finds that choice options in healthcare are important. Sometimes the difference in rates is so large or the quality so low that it is undesirable for every care provider to be financed by the municipality.

215944.explanation: The CDA sees period poverty as a serious social problem that hinders equal opportunities and social participation of women and girls. We choose concrete solutions and support national funding for free products for those in need.

215945.explanation: The CDA favors careful citizen participation per topic.

215949.explanation: This depends on the company's activity and the environment.

215951.explanation: This promotes awareness of the importance of waste separation.

215952.explanation: The CDA supports the Spread Act. This law ensures that every municipality makes its proportional contribution to the reception of refugees.

215954.explanation: Unless it is at a dog walking area designated by the municipality.

215955.explanation: The substances used have undergone a strict procedure before they can be applied as crop protection.

215956.explanation: There must be sufficient greenery in the neighborhoods. Even in new residential areas, there must be adequate attention to green spaces within the neighborhood.

215957.explanation: Tourism is an important pillar in the rural area.

215958.explanation: This promotes tourism and traffic safety. We have been advocating for years for a safe cycling connection from De Pollen to Bruinehaar.

215960.explanation: Culture and art are important for education and development.

215963.explanation: Banning drinking on the street prevents nuisance. This excludes, of course, festivals, events, and terraces.

215964.explanation: A fine alone is not the solution. However, dialogue with the owner is necessary to find a solution and to make clear agreements to resolve the issue quickly.

215966.explanation: This improves traffic flow and helps address the housing shortage.

215968.explanation: The Canal Zone is the responsibility of the province.

215971.explanation: This is for the safety of cyclists (think, for example, of the sports parks and accessibility of schools).

215973.explanation: We must focus especially on prevention. Youth workers are very important both for young people facing these problems and certainly also for other youth.

215974.explanation: This can prevent further escalation. Neighborhood mediation is a good initiative from the municipality, which we fully support.

215975.explanation: This prevents children with learning disadvantages from entering primary school and works preventively.

215976.explanation: There are currently enough enforcement officers.

215918.explanation: We do not want extra taxes for dog owners. However, in our opinion, strict enforcement is needed regarding nuisance caused by dog feces.

215920.explanation: Because Sunday openings are permitted in Twenterand, we believe that shopkeepers should not be forced to open. The day of rest is important to us.

215921.explanation: We want agriculture and nature to be in balance, without additional regulations beyond national policy.

215922.explanation: Municipalities share responsibility for refugee reception. People with a residence permit must be treated equally as other housing seekers. Priority creates inequality; better distribution and more affordable housing are, in our opinion, the best solutions.

215923.explanation: Housing for workers from other countries must be well arranged. Companies have their responsibility. Municipalities and provinces must include this in their housing vision. The goal is safe and humane housing with clear rules.

215924.explanation: By disposing of waste at the recycling center, we contribute to better waste separation. The polluter pays, which is fair.

215925.explanation: When mowing public grass, safety and livability are paramount. In places where mowing is not necessary, we give space to biodiversity.

215926.explanation: Festivals are moments of connection and meeting, but noise should not cause too much nuisance. We want clear rules.

215927.explanation: We support local energy initiatives and provide opportunities for citizen initiatives. We follow the national solar panel ladder, which places agricultural land near the bottom for use for solar panels.

215928.explanation: Sports are important for health, social contacts, and participation in society. Children must not be excluded because their parents have little money. Therefore, we want the municipality to continue helping with membership fees.

215929.explanation: Every child should be able to swim by the end of primary school. We encourage attention to school swimming for this reason.

215930.explanation: In our view, all children receive music lessons at school, supported by the municipality. Children from low-income families must also be able to participate in society. Therefore, we want to explore whether additional lessons are possible with subsidies or financial aid.

215932.explanation: Affordable housing is important. We consider the needs of our residents. Social rental housing is important in this regard.

215933.explanation: We do not automatically allow housing on industrial estates. We look where there is demand, where it is safe, and where it improves livability.

215934.explanation: Holiday homes in parks are intended for recreation. In other places, where the situation allows, permanent residence can be considered.

215935.explanation: We want to combat poverty and offer tailored support. The Money Worries contact point plays an important role in our municipality.

215936.explanation: Everyone should have the chance to fully participate in society. Through mandatory volunteer work or (work-oriented) day activities, people can be fully engaged in society. It provides self-worth, satisfaction, and a place in society.

215937.explanation: People with little money should not get into trouble because of taxes. Where necessary, we consider support options. Automatic exemption based on low income is, in our opinion, not the solution.

215938.explanation: Informal caregivers deserve good support. We do not want to burden them additionally. Therefore, we look at individual needs and provide appropriate support. That does not automatically mean that as a caregiver you do not have to apply for jobs.

215939.explanation: We want to stimulate sustainability. More charging points help residents choose electric driving sooner.

215940.explanation: Lower speeds make streets safer, especially for children and cyclists. Where safety is at risk, the maximum speed must be lowered. Enforcement in current zones is the starting point.

215941.explanation: We stand by our entrepreneurs. Even when they face financial problems.

215942.explanation: Respect for religion and culture is important in healthcare. Everyone is unique and therefore free to choose.

215944.explanation: We want to combat poverty. That is why we provide free menstrual products at places frequented by vulnerable groups, such as schools, food banks, and care points.

215945.explanation: We want residents to think along and participate more. We value consultation, but first want to see if existing forms are working well.

215949.explanation: Companies are important for jobs and the economy. If a company fits well in the rural area, we believe the municipality should enable this. This way, there remains space for entrepreneurship and a livable rural area.

215951.explanation: Waste separation should be rewarding. A lower fixed fee and higher payment per disposal.

215952.explanation: Every municipality has a task in sheltering. This is prescribed by law. We want to care for people in need. The municipality has done this before and we think it fits our values: hospitality and responsibility.

215953.explanation: We want small-scale shelters. A location for a maximum of 50 people is a good starting point.

215954.explanation: Dog poop is the biggest nuisance in the municipality. The municipality has already done a lot, for example by placing more trash bins. Now it is the dog owners' task to clean up poop in the built-up area. A fine can help ensure everyone complies.

215955.explanation: We want to care for nature and health. Less agricultural pesticide use is part of that. We do not add extra rules to national policy but encourage less use of pesticides and sustainable alternatives.

215956.explanation: We focus on more greenery. Greenery is good for health and the climate.

215957.explanation: We want to stimulate recreation, balancing entrepreneurship and nature.

215958.explanation: Cycling is healthy and must be safe. We want more cycle paths, especially outside the villages.

215960.explanation: Art and culture connect people. We want to preserve these. Associations should receive financial support, such as music clubs.

215963.explanation: Alcohol on the street often causes nuisance. We do not want this and therefore prohibit it.

215964.explanation: Vacancy is bad for villages. We see this, for example, in Vriezenveen. Fines help get buildings back into use. This contributes to housing opportunities and availability.

215966.explanation: We look for ways to promote housing market turnover. A moving allowance for the elderly is one of the possibilities.

215968.explanation: We want the canal zone to become an attractive place to live, work, and recreate. Renovating this area strengthens livability. The province plays an important role, and as a municipality, we look for ways to support this well.

215971.explanation: Cyclists must be safe. Designing bicycle streets ensures calmer traffic.

215973.explanation: Our municipality struggles with drug issues. Young people who are addicted need help. More youth workers mean they get help faster and are not left alone.

215974.explanation: Conflicts between neighbors can escalate. Volunteer mediators help find solutions together. This prevents problems and ensures peace.

215975.explanation: Every child must be able to play and learn. If parents have little money, the municipality helps. This way, every child gets a good start.

215976.explanation: Safety is important. More enforcement officers ensure rules are followed and people feel safe.

215918.explanation: Why tax dogs and not other pets? This is unfair. Moreover, a specific tax costs more to administer than it yields.

215920.explanation: The SGP supports promoting Sunday rest. Sunday is a God-given day of rest that must be protected. In this hectic time, a common day of rest for families, (small) entrepreneurs, and society is a great blessing.

215921.explanation: We must be careful with our precious agricultural land. Our farmers provide our food and keep our rural area livable.

215922.explanation: Why should we give priority to others? Our youth, born and raised here, also deserve priority for housing!

215923.explanation: Companies and workers are themselves responsible for housing. However, the municipality must provide good policies so entrepreneurs can let these workers work and live safely and decently.

215924.explanation: Free sounds good, but the principle is: the polluter pays. The SGP supports low taxes and fees. Garden waste must remain free. This also applies to items for which residents have already paid a disposal contribution.

215925.explanation: Public green spaces (such as sidewalks, parks, etc.) MUST be maintained much better. This needs improvement. Moreover, mowing must consider the growth and flowering periods!

215926.explanation: Increasing nuisance is being experienced. Too many decibels cause disturbance to residents and hearing damage to visitors. Events should be possible and are good, but we must set limits and enforce them. We must also consider specific event locations.

215927.explanation: Land is scarce! We must be careful with our valuable agricultural land. Our farmers provide our food. Solar panels belong on roofs! There is still enough roof space in our municipality!

215928.explanation: We must handle public money carefully. There are municipal arrangements for people living below the poverty line so that we can provide help where it is REALLY needed.

215929.explanation: This is not a core task of the municipality. The SGP finds it important that children learn to swim. This can be encouraged in various ways. Schools manage their own timetables and lesson offerings. The SGP supports promoting swimming lessons by financially supporting families.

215930.explanation: This is not a core task of the municipality. Music lessons are given at school, and further it is a choice and responsibility of the parent(s)/guardian(s).

215932.explanation: Building must be based on need! We should not focus solely on percentages. The SGP believes free plots should also be available so people can build themselves!

215933.explanation: There must be space for businesses with housing in our municipality. It is a very nice transition from residential area to business park. And it is good for social control at a business park.

215934.explanation: Holiday homes are meant for vacation guests, right?! However, the SGP believes customized solutions should be applied here. Because there are different situations that require solutions. Think, for example, of deteriorated holiday parks, etc.

215935.explanation: This is a task for the national government! Of course, the SGP wants to help people who are in need or poverty!

215936.explanation: Residents receiving benefits may, according to the SGP, be required to provide a 'return service' when somewhat possible. This can be in the form of volunteer work, for example.

215937.explanation: The scheme exists. We are cautious about this, but the municipality has the possibility to waive taxes and must retain this option. However, this must be justifiable and cost-conscious.

215938.explanation: Caregivers should be supported where necessary. But even a caregiver benefits from (partial) work outside the home (balance between work and care). Considering the human dimension is the most important aspect here.

215939.explanation: There are more and more electric cars. This also applies to (electric) bicycles! The municipality must respond to this in public spaces.

215940.explanation: Let's look at the local situation. It is much more important to involve the neighborhood in the wishes to improve safety and traffic flow.

215941.explanation: That is only fair. However, only when it concerns private debts! Entrepreneurs should also be able to apply for debt assistance while retaining their business (just like employees with a regular job). All this under conditions! The community cannot bear all costs!

215942.explanation: Freedom of choice is important for recognizability and understanding. It helps people feel better, safer, and understood, leading to better treatment outcomes.

215944.explanation: This is national policy for health insurers. This is arbitrary-why distribute these products for free and not other items?

215945.explanation: Involvement of our residents is good. What is important is to provide clear information and transparency in advance. Ultimately, the municipal council is responsible for the decision to be made. A careful consideration is essential.

215949.explanation: The municipality should adopt a service-oriented attitude. That does not mean that everything is allowed. Not every business or activity should be permitted in the rural area. The key is how we keep our rural area livable. The farming businesses established here over the years must not be hindered by this.

215951.explanation: Taxes should be kept as low as possible. Our residents and entrepreneurs are not cash cows. The polluter pays principle is justifiable. However, we must avoid undesirable situations that encourage people to illegally dump their waste.

215952.explanation: The SGP is not in favor of (large) asylum reception centers in Twenterand. It is unacceptable that our youth cannot find housing. Let's first ensure housing for our young people. Naturally, the SGP wants to help refugees and people in need.

215953.explanation: The SGP is not in favor of (large) asylum reception centers in Twenterand. It is unacceptable that our youth cannot find housing. Let's first ensure housing for our young people.

215954.explanation: We must keep public spaces clean. This must be enforced. The SGP supports sufficient dog toilets at logical locations, agreed upon with the neighborhood or district, and regularly cleaned.

215955.explanation: What a misleading term to call it agricultural poison. No one wants poison. These are carefully selected crop protection products, including for potato cultivation. National and European laws apply. We should, however, explore possibilities for zoning and careful use in consultation.

215956.explanation: Green spaces are important and good for a healthy and pleasant living environment. If we leave everything to developers, every inch gets built over. That should be avoided.

215957.explanation: We must cherish our beautiful green countryside, where our farmers produce our food and manage the landscape. Adding other businesses requires a case-by-case approach. We support fewer rules and clarity. You can't want everything everywhere. The SGP advocates for a livable rural area.

215958.explanation: Certainly, where safety is at stake, the municipality must take action. It is good for residents and tourists to enjoy our beautiful countryside.

215960.explanation: Art and culture are important for meaning and connection. However, it is not a core task of the municipality.

215963.explanation: This is regulated in the General Municipal By-laws (APV), except for terraces and hospitality venues. Or when a specific permit has been issued. And that is good.

215964.explanation: The municipality has no authority over private properties. There can be various reasons for issues. However, the municipality must intervene when deterioration or unsafe situations arise.

215966.explanation: Where do you start? Let's ensure there are enough homes. What use is compensation if no houses are available?

215968.explanation: Absolutely. The municipality must collaborate with the province and residents along the canal. The province has already allocated several million euros. The municipality cannot fall behind and must invest in a livable and safe residential area along the Almelo-de Haandrik canal.

215971.explanation: That could be possible, but it must be done in consultation with the involved entrepreneurs, residents, and property owners. The SGP believes investment must be made in livability, safety, and attractive shopping centers in ALL localities.

215973.explanation: Investing in youth is good, but not only in troubled youth. Let's also invest in young people who perform well and work hard for their money. Of course, the SGP supports good education regarding addiction. Much is already being done on this.

215974.explanation: This is not a core task of the municipality. Why should the municipality get involved in neighbor disputes? However, in some cases, the municipality can lend a helping hand when needed and when it benefits neighborhood livability.

215975.explanation: Parents are already nationally compensated for childcare costs. The SGP believes the municipality should look at and help where it is truly necessary.

215976.explanation: Enforcers are not only needed for safety but also for supervision and enforcement of other types of nuisance.

215918.explanation: Revenues from the dog tax are often used by municipalities for purposes unrelated to dogs. The PVV therefore sees introducing the dog tax as an unnecessary increase in costs.

215920.explanation: The municipalities surrounding Twenterand all have Sunday shopping. If shops in Twenterand must close on Sundays, this will cause unfair competition.

215921.explanation: Farmers already have a hard time in the Netherlands. Twenterand should be proud of its farmers and certainly not convert fertile agricultural land into nature reserves.

215922.explanation: The PVV wants our youth in Twenterand to find housing. Granting priority to status holders creates unfair competition.

215923.explanation: When an entrepreneur employs guest workers, the costs for housing these people should not fall on the residents of Twenterand.

215924.explanation: The PVV wants the first 100 kilos to be free for private individuals.

215925.explanation: Twenterand currently follows a mowing policy with minimal mowing. In many places this is fine, but some spots result in difficult, unclear intersections (due to tall grass) and an untidy appearance in neighborhoods. The PVV wants more frequent mowing in these locations.

215926.explanation: The rules for events are already very strict. As a municipality, we should support the volunteers who organize these events, not burden them with even more regulations.

215927.explanation: We have some of the most fertile farmland in the world in the Netherlands. The PVV believes solar panels belong on roofs, not on our valuable agricultural land.

215928.explanation: It is important that every child can participate in sports. When parents cannot afford this, the municipality should help.

215929.explanation: For the safety of our children, it is important each child learns to swim. School swimming programs can assist with this.

215930.explanation: It is great when children have the opportunity to play an instrument, but since the municipality unfortunately cannot fund everything, PVV Twenterand prefers children to engage in sports.

215932.explanation: There is a need for affordable housing in Twenterand. PVV Twenterand therefore wants more affordable houses to be built.

215933.explanation: PVV Twenterand believes entrepreneurs should be able to build a residence next to their business premises. However, it is not intended to create residential neighborhoods in industrial areas.

215934.explanation: There is a severe housing shortage. The PVV therefore wants to allow permanent residence in holiday homes.

215935.explanation: The current provisions for low-income individuals are specifically intended for those who truly need them. The PVV wants work to pay off again, so people retain more of their income and fewer people need these provisions.

215936.explanation: As far as the PVV is concerned, everyone in the Netherlands should contribute. Of course, this can also be volunteer work.

215937.explanation: The PVV does not want our residents who currently use these services to get into financial trouble. Stopping debt forgiveness without providing an alternative solution is not acceptable to the PVV.

215938.explanation: The PVV is proud of our informal caregivers and recognizes that this can be such a demanding task that working becomes impossible.

215939.explanation: The PVV believes this responsibility does not lie with the municipality. The creation of charging points must be done by our entrepreneurs and residents themselves.

215940.explanation: In 99% of cases, the 50 km/h speed limit within built-up areas is appropriate. When a 30 km/h limit is more suitable on a specific road, we will adjust it there, but we will not do this everywhere in Twenterand.

215941.explanation: PVV Twenterand sees entrepreneurs as the driving force of our society. As a municipality, we must always assist our entrepreneurs so they can focus on what they do best: entrepreneurship.

215942.explanation: PVV Twenterand has always committed to good care for our residents. Being able to choose a healthcare provider that fits your faith, culture, and beliefs is a completely unnecessary and unfeasible demand.

215944.explanation: Residents who cannot afford these products can go to the food bank.

215945.explanation: A citizens' assembly based on sortition is not democratically elected and therefore should have no place in our democracy.

215949.explanation: PVV Twenterand wants the municipality to provide space to entrepreneurs. When an entrepreneur wants to establish in the rural area, the municipality must cooperate.

215951.explanation: PVV Twenterand believes that large households are disadvantaged as a result.

215952.explanation: PVV Twenterand has always been strongly opposed to an asylum seekers' center (azc) in Twenterand. Thanks in part to our efforts, Twenterand has not received an azc.

215953.explanation: PVV Twenterand does not want to accommodate any asylum seekers in Twenterand at all.

215954.explanation: Dog poop is a major annoyance for many of our residents. PVV Twenterand wants to take action against people who deliberately break the rules.

215955.explanation: PVV Twenterand believes that rules about agricultural chemicals should not be made by the municipality but by the Province or The Hague.

215956.explanation: PVV Twenterand believes that in some places in the municipality there should be more space for trees and greenery, but in other places trees should be cut down.

215957.explanation: Tourism is a good source of income for our municipality. PVV Twenterand therefore welcomes more enthusiastic entrepreneurs to start a business in the recreation sector.

215958.explanation: PVV Twenterand wants children in rural areas to be able to cycle to school safely, and bike paths can help achieve this.

215960.explanation: According to the PVV, everyone must be able to support themselves financially, including artists. However, the PVV wants to ensure that facilities such as the library can always remain open.

215963.explanation: PVV Twenterand is always against unnecessary regulations. During events and holidays such as King's Day and New Year's Eve, having fun on the streets should simply be allowed. In case of nuisance, the municipality can still intervene.

215964.explanation: Long-vacant buildings such as the old Rabobank in Vriezenveen become dilapidated and are not a good showcase for our municipality.

215966.explanation: The PVV wants young people to be able to stay living in Twenterand. When seniors leave their large family homes, this means a house becomes available for a young family.

215968.explanation: This is the task of the Province and not of the municipality of Twenterand.

215971.explanation: If necessary, a 30 km/h zone is sufficient.

215973.explanation: Youth workers in Twenterand do fantastic work. As far as the PVV is concerned, this should be further expanded.

215974.explanation: We want to be a pleasant municipality to live in, and neighborhood disputes can have a significant impact on a neighborhood. Volunteer mediators can help resolve conflicts.

215975.explanation: For the PVV, it is important that all children get the same opportunities and can go to primary school with a good foundation.

215976.explanation: We want Twenterand to be a municipality where everyone feels safe. Hiring more enforcement officers (BOAs) can have a positive impact here.

215918.explanation: Many residents in Twenterand are annoyed by dog poop and that it is not cleaned up. Through a dog tax, waste bins, designated dog walking areas, and dog parks can be created, and enforcement officers can be paid to enforce the rules.

215920.explanation: Maintain the current number of shopping Sundays and give shopkeepers the freedom and responsibility to organize them.

215921.explanation: There are Natura 2000 areas in Twenterand (Engbertsdijksvenen) and to preserve and develop these, it may be necessary to use agricultural land differently (extensively) or convert it to nature.

215922.explanation: Every municipality has a task in housing refugees. Twenterand can, together with Mijande, prioritize certain groups (divorced couples, assisted living, and refugees). In Twenterand, refugee housing concerns only 5 to 7 homes per year.

215923.explanation: Companies are responsible for housing labor migrants and must comply with the SNF quality mark. Exploitation of labor migrants must be opposed at all times, and the municipality must intervene where necessary.

215924.explanation: Efforts must be made to separate waste as much as possible, also at the recycling center and, where possible, free of charge. However, waste processing costs must be covered.

215925.explanation: Many residents in Twenterand are annoyed with the greenery maintenance in Twenterand. In consultation with them, a plan must be made for greenery maintenance. It is important to also consider maintenance with high biodiversity.

215926.explanation: Currently, Twenterand has no regulations for festivals regarding noise standards. It is important to look into this in consultation with festival organizers and neighbors.

215927.explanation: Solar and wind energy are important for the future. Therefore, we want to explore more solar energy on land, as there are good ways to combine arable and grassland with solar panels.

215928.explanation: Exercise and sports are important for everyone. Barriers for children from vulnerable families to join sports clubs will be removed. Lack of money should never be a reason not to play sports. We also offer targeted programs for adults and the elderly.

215929.explanation: Every child should be able to swim, and therefore we want to offer school swimming lessons to all students in group 5.

215930.explanation: Culture and music are of great importance for a child's development. We advocate for extending the school day so that children can be introduced to music, art, and culture, allowing them to expand their talents.

215932.explanation: New construction must be varied. This also means investing in and building in the social rental sector. At least 30% of new construction must be social rental housing.

215933.explanation: Newly built homes should primarily be realized in existing centers, neighborhoods, and within the built-up areas. Additionally, new neighborhoods on the edges of these centers should fit the character of each center.

215934.explanation: Permanent living in vacation homes is not a sustainable solution for the housing shortage. They are not suitable for this, but can be used as temporary shelter.

215935.explanation: Everyone must have opportunities and possibilities to participate. This means that people with minimum incomes (up to 120% of social assistance) should be able to receive support.

215936.explanation: Participation is important for everyone. The focus should especially be on work (basic jobs), and volunteer work can be a good stepping stone. People participate according to their ability, which requires tailor-made solutions. Compulsion is not the approach.

215937.explanation: Residents with minimum incomes must be able to participate according to their ability. Lack of money should never be a reason not to participate. This means that some residents in Twenterand must continue to have access to debt remission.

215938.explanation: There is an increasing demand for informal care and the involvement of informal caregivers, which often requires a lot of time. During the period of informal care, the obligation to apply for jobs should be lifted. Tailor-made solutions will be provided.

215939.explanation: Twenterand must specifically designate and develop locations for charging electric cars. Currently, there are too few places with charging stations in Twenterand where this is possible.

215940.explanation: We want to improve traffic safety. We will ensure that, where possible, the maximum speed is reduced to 30 km per hour so that cyclists and pedestrians are safer on the streets.

215941.explanation: Where possible, entrepreneurs should also be supported in debt counseling. This requires tailor-made solutions (in compliance with laws and regulations).

215942.explanation: Freedom of choice is important. Care providers must meet established quality requirements and be a party contracted by the municipality.

215944.explanation: Being and staying healthy is important, and hygiene also matters. The municipality does not have to provide this itself but can support the relevant organizations by supplying these resources.

215945.explanation: Citizen assemblies are a strong and complementary instrument for involving residents in discussing, thinking about, and advising on important themes affecting Twenterand.

215949.explanation: Companies should establish themselves in the available and developed business parks where space and expansion opportunities are offered to them.

215951.explanation: As much as possible, efforts should be made to separate waste to reduce costs. Waste separation should be the focus and is going well in Twenterand. Residents pay basic costs collectively and thereafter based on the waste they dispose of.

215952.explanation: The municipality of Twenterand currently does not implement the Distribution Act. It does have a duty and obligation to do so, which includes accommodating asylum seekers.

215953.explanation: In accommodating asylum seekers (Distribution Act), consideration must be given to support and capacity. If this can be done on a small scale (maximum 50 asylum seekers per location), this is preferred.

215954.explanation: The principle 'clean up your own mess' applies here, and if this is not done, a fine can be imposed.

215955.explanation: We protect our residents against agricultural pesticides. We want to ban as many pesticides as possible and establish buffer zones around homes, schools, and nature areas. We want to explore the possibilities of banning ornamental and lily cultivation.

215956.explanation: We want to work on the principle of the 3 - 30 - 300 rule. This means residents can see 3 mature trees from their home window, 30% of the area is covered by a canopy, and there is a park within 300 meters.

215957.explanation: Tourism and recreation are important in Twenterand. It is important to work nature-inclusive and connect well with the natural areas.

215958.explanation: Good cycling connections within Twenterand enhance traffic safety and improve public health by increasing the number of cyclists.

215960.explanation: Art and culture are important for every resident of Twenterand. They contribute to the development of young and old and also provide relaxation. Currently, too few resources are allocated to this in Twenterand.

215963.explanation: A ban on drinking alcohol on the street is unenforceable. It is better to invest in preventing excessive alcohol consumption, especially among youth.

215964.explanation: Given the current housing shortage, it is important to quickly give vacant buildings a new purpose and use. When buildings are deliberately left empty (speculation), the municipality must be able to take action.

215966.explanation: Efforts to encourage older residents to move from family homes to, for example, apartments should be stimulated. This can be done by giving priority during allocation and/or providing resources.

215968.explanation: Together with the Province of Overijssel, the municipality of Twenterand must invest in the area along the Almelo-De Haandrik Canal. There must be a comprehensive approach for housing (sustainable), economy, and social cohesion. This must be done together with the residents.

215971.explanation: Important for traffic safety, especially in residential neighborhoods.

215973.explanation: The municipality must invest in youth work but should not employ youth workers itself. The municipality should focus on prevention, but here too, professionals should carry this out.

215974.explanation: Efforts in voluntary neighborhood mediation have proven their value and should be further developed. The sooner neighborhood mediation can be deployed, the sooner escalation can be prevented.

215975.explanation: Preschool and early school education are important. Every child between 2 and 6 years old should have access to extra language education and developmental activities. It is important to invest in development and education in Twenterand.

215976.explanation: Creating regulations also requires enforcement. This is important for support. Residents must see that those who do not comply with rules are also subject to enforcement.

215918.explanation: This was recently abolished and will not be reinstated.

215920.explanation: Shops have only recently been allowed to open longer. This is a positive development. The VVD even supports allowing them to stay open longer.

215921.explanation: We should not further restrict the agricultural sector.

215922.explanation: No priority, but simply join the queue like everyone else.

215923.explanation: This should be addressed jointly. There are examples within the municipality of housing that work well. This can be resolved by employment agencies, employers, or in cooperation with the municipality.

215924.explanation: The polluter pays, but the fee should be as low as possible.

215925.explanation: That is a choice made by the municipal council based on costs. More frequent mowing means higher costs.

215926.explanation: We find the current standards sufficient. Festivals occur only sporadically in the municipality.

215927.explanation: Encourage solar panels on existing roofs.

215928.explanation: There is a good arrangement for that now, which we want to maintain.

215929.explanation: School swimming should be part of the curriculum again.

215930.explanation: There is currently a subsidy up to age 18, keeping it accessible for every child and not just the elite.

215932.explanation: But we do need to look at changes in housing demand. So aligning with the actual needs.

215933.explanation: If necessary, a company residence should be allowed.

215934.explanation: Yes, that solves part of the housing shortage in the short term. If a house is suitable as a holiday home, it is also suitable for permanent residence.

215935.explanation: There are already many arrangements for low-income people. Twenterand already has a higher income threshold than the national level.

215936.explanation: Some return can be expected. That also creates employment opportunities.

215937.explanation: We can maintain the current arrangements. Potential debt forgiveness is part of a broader assistance package.

215938.explanation: The job application obligation always applies. There are also many people who are informal caregivers and still have regular work.

215939.explanation: Everyone can charge at home themselves. Charging points are not a municipal task. The municipality also does not operate gas stations.

215940.explanation: That depends on where and how the road is designed. There are roads in Twenterand that need to be adapted.

215941.explanation: Especially self-employed people can get into trouble and may need help to get out of it.

215942.explanation: There is a broad range of various types of care related to faith, etc. However, there are limitations. You cannot offer everything or please everyone completely. There is a financial aspect to this, and healthcare costs must be controlled.

215944.explanation: The municipality should not act as a health insurer.

215945.explanation: That is a good idea in itself, but the initiative must come from the population. There have been several attempts to set up some kind of citizen council, but they have always ended after a short time.

215949.explanation: Provided this is possible within the zoning plan or it makes sense to amend the zoning plan for that location.

215951.explanation: This is better in principle for the 'polluter pays' principle, but there are limitations. There are costs that must be generally distributed to maintain the entire waste system.

215952.explanation: That depends. We now have an agreement with other municipalities that take in a higher quota of asylum seekers, and Twenterand a higher number of Ukrainians. So it can be organized differently.

215953.explanation: That entirely depends on the number arriving and the composition. You treat a group of young people differently than a few families.

215954.explanation: Dog poop is the biggest nuisance and simply antisocial.

215955.explanation: This must be aligned with national policy. You cannot impose a different regime on existing entrepreneurs in Twenterand than elsewhere. That would create unfair competition. You can set restrictions for new businesses.

215956.explanation: Planting trees is good for climate adaptation and absorbing rainwater. Much can still be done here. Planting trees is one way.

215957.explanation: Looking at where it is possible and facilitating it is better than opposing this kind of development. Tourism is a good sector of the economy and benefits local businesses.

215958.explanation: Where this can be included cost-wise with other plans, we should definitely include it. For example, towards Bruinehaar we should address this.

215960.explanation: Art and culture are important for human development. Churchill already said that art and culture are essential for a complete life and should be supported.

215963.explanation: If that becomes a problem, yes. But if that is not the case, do not create new rules.

215964.explanation: There can be many reasons for vacancy. The municipality can engage in dialogue and encourage buildings to be used or cooperate with zoning changes.

215966.explanation: This is not a municipality task. Moving costs are taken into account during sales.

215968.explanation: This is not a municipal task. Moreover, you should consider whether it is wise to build more houses here.

215971.explanation: This can be applied in various village centers, but the car remains important for a rural municipality like Twenterand.

215973.explanation: The approach to drugs and addiction has a high priority. Various organizations are involved. Whether more youth workers are needed for this will have to be assessed.

215974.explanation: Mediators can be a useful tool in neighbor disputes and often prevent much worse suffering through mediation.

215975.explanation: Plans are underway to make this free. This is national policy and should not be arranged independently by the municipality.

215976.explanation: We recently increased the number of BOA's (special enforcement officers). First, we wait to see if this is sufficient. If necessary, the VVD is not against more BOA's, but it is not immediately needed now.

215918.explanation: The dog tax must not return. It mainly affects families and generates little revenue.

215920.explanation: Shopkeepers should decide themselves whether to open on Sundays. Residents choose whether or not to make use of this.

215921.explanation: We support nature conservation, but agricultural land is meant for our farmers. The municipality must support farmers, not work against them.

215922.explanation: Social rental homes are scarce. Priority should go to residents who have lived here for years and are waiting for a home.

215923.explanation: Employers who employ labor migrants are responsible for adequate housing themselves. Housing in Twenterand is meant for our residents, not to solve companies' problems.

215924.explanation: The rates at the recycling center are too high and must be lowered. Residents should receive a free basic quota, for example up to 100 kilos. Not everything has to be free.

215925.explanation: Public green spaces are in good condition. The municipality must ensure that this continues to be the case.

215926.explanation: Events belong to a vibrant Twenterand. With good information provided to local residents, understanding arises and nuisance can be limited.

215927.explanation: Our landscape is not an industrial area. Wind turbines and solar parks do not fit here. Insulation and solar panels on roofs take priority over large projects in the countryside.

215928.explanation: Children should grow up safely and without poverty in Twenterand. When families struggle to keep up, the municipality supports them with school, sports, and activities so every child can participate.

215929.explanation: Our villages are rich in water. Therefore, it is essential that all children learn to swim well.

215930.explanation: Hobbies such as music lessons are a personal choice.

215932.explanation: This is not a solution to the housing shortage; there is a lack of social rental homes and affordable owner-occupied homes.

215933.explanation: Business parks are intended for work. Living there does not belong, except where a company residence is necessary for the entrepreneur.

215934.explanation: This reduces pressure on the housing market.

215935.explanation: Raising the income limit is not a targeted solution. Help should go to residents who truly need it.

215936.explanation: We support guiding people back to the labor market. Social work is a stepping stone, not a final destination.

215937.explanation: Debt forgiveness must remain available for those who need it, but with clear rules and good control.

215938.explanation: Informal care is important but should only be used when truly necessary and actually provided. Customized solutions are essential here.

215939.explanation: Those who own an electric car must provide their own charging station. There are sufficient public charging points in Twenterand, and extra charging spots can be added near residential care centers if needed.

215940.explanation: Traffic safety must be assessed street by street.

215941.explanation: Debt assistance should be possible if a company is viable. This way we retain local jobs and entrepreneurs without wasting money on companies without a future.

215942.explanation: Complaints about care belong with the care institution; this is not a municipal responsibility.

215944.explanation: Free menstrual products must be available for those who need them, through schools and aid organizations. The municipality does not distribute these widely but facilitates so they reach the right institutions.

215945.explanation: Twenterand already has good village councils. An additional citizens' assembly is not necessary.

215949.explanation: The countryside must remain protected, but customized solutions are possible in special cases. Not everything can be allowed, but not everything needs to be forbidden either.

215951.explanation: The price per waste bin must not increase further. Residents should not be additionally burdened for their waste.

215952.explanation: Twenterand already hosts Ukrainian refugees. We do not want asylum seeker accommodations in our municipality.

215953.explanation: Twenterand already hosts Ukrainian refugees. We do not want asylum seeker accommodations in our municipality.

215954.explanation: The municipality must provide more dog waste bins; we expect dog owners to take responsibility.

215955.explanation: This is not a municipal task. The use and approval of agricultural pesticides is national policy.

215956.explanation: Greenery is important for the livability within our villages.

215957.explanation: Allow where it fits the area.

215958.explanation: First prioritize the maintenance and lighting of existing bike paths, then consider new ones.

215960.explanation: The municipality should focus on core tasks.

215963.explanation: This is not an issue of new policy but of enforcing existing rules. The municipality must simply do what has already been agreed upon.

215964.explanation: Vacancies harm neighborhoods and waste housing space. Encourage first; enforce only if unwillingness persists.

215966.explanation: The problem is not the allowance, but the lack of suitable smaller homes.

215968.explanation: The province is working on repairing the damage. This area needs rest. First restore and renovate, no new interventions.

215971.explanation: Traffic measures must be examined per street. Bike streets only where they truly fit and improve safety.

215973.explanation: Addiction care requires professional help. Specialized institutions exist for this, not extra youth workers.

215974.explanation: Rapid deployment of volunteer mediators can prevent escalation and keep neighbor disputes manageable.

215975.explanation: The preschool playgroup must be accessible to all children.

215976.explanation: There are currently four enforcement officers (BOAs), which is sufficient; however, we want them to be more visibly present on the streets. We prefer that the police station be staffed more often rather than appointing additional BOAs.

statements.title: The municipality must ban drinking alcohol on the street.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: Twenterand must have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who deal with safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.
Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: ) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should mow public grass more often.
The municipality must fine owners who leave a building vacant for longer than a year.
Entrepreneurs must be able to apply for debt counseling while keeping their business.

titleParts.glossary: The municipality helps people get out of debt. Sometimes they only receive advice. Sometimes the municipality helps make arrangements with creditors. Sometimes they receive an amount to live on and the rest of their income goes to their creditors.

titleParts.text: kunnen aanvragen met behoud van hun bedrijf.

statements.title: The municipality must cooperate in the reception/sheltering of asylum seekers.
Housing should also be built on business parks.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality should distribute tampons and sanitary pads for free.
Owners who do not clean up their dog's poop must always receive a fine.
The municipality of Twenterand must invest more money in renovating the canal zone (Almelo–De Haandrik canal), for example with housing developments.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality does not have to plant extra trees within the built-up area.

titleParts.text: De gemeente hoeft geen extra bomen te planten binnen de

titleParts.glossary: An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: Twenterand must establish a citizens' assembly that advises the municipal council. A citizens' assembly consists of residents chosen by lot.

titleParts.glossary: A group of residents chosen by lot that advises politicians or helps decide on a specific issue.

titleParts.text: krijgen, dat advies geeft aan de gemeenteraad. In een

titleParts.glossary: A group of residents chosen by lot that advises politicians or helps decide on a specific issue.

titleParts.text: zitten inwoners die door loting zijn uitgekozen.

statements.title: The municipality must make some streets bicycle-priority streets where cars must drive slowly and carefully.
If companies want to locate in the rural area, the municipality must cooperate.

titleParts.glossary: Area within the municipality's borders but outside the towns or villages themselves; there are few houses and other buildings here.
To build; to establish or set up (a building, organization or household).

statements.title: The municipality must hire more youth workers who help addicted young people.

titleParts.glossary: People who help young people in the municipality with various matters.

titleParts.text: in dienst nemen die verslaafde jongeren helpen.

statements.title: There must be a relocation allowance for elderly people who leave a large family home.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

titleParts.text: krijgen, moeten in ruil daarvoor maatschappelijk zinvol werk doen.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: Shops in Twenterand must be closed on Sundays.
Campsites and other businesses that attract tourists must be able to establish themselves more easily in Twenterand's rural area.

titleParts.text: Campings en andere bedrijven die toeristen trekken, moeten zich makkelijker in het

titleParts.glossary: Area within the municipality's borders but outside the towns or villages themselves; there are few houses and other buildings here.
To build; to establish or set up (a building, organization or household).

statements.title: At least 30% of newly built homes in Twenterand must be social rental housing. Currently 25% of homes in Twenterand are social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Twenterand moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. Op dit moment is 25% van de woningen in Twenterand een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality must spend less money on art and culture.
The annual waste collection charge must be lowered and the price per waste bin must be raised.

titleParts.glossary: Municipal tax for collecting and processing household waste.

titleParts.text: moet omlaag en de prijs per afvalbak moet omhoog.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
Dropping off waste at the recycling center should be free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: The municipality must introduce a dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

shootoutStatements.title: Companies that employ workers from other countries must provide housing for them themselves.
People receiving welfare benefits who provide informal care to a family member or acquaintance do not have to apply for jobs.
The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality should house asylum seekers in groups of no more than 50.
The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.
The municipality should contribute to preschool/playgroup costs for children of low-income parents.
The municipality should provide more cycle paths in rural areas.
The municipality should prevent agricultural land in Twenterand from being converted into nature areas.
The municipality should ban the use of pesticides in bulb cultivation.
The municipality should support more people in poverty by raising the income threshold for assistance.
The municipality should deploy voluntary mediators more quickly in neighbor disputes.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.
Noise limits at festivals should be stricter.
The municipality should continue to pay sports club memberships for children whose parents or caregivers have little money.
